name: aios-glass
description: A friendly glass layer over the AIOS — run rituals, browse agents, manage spaces, all from inside Antigravity. Glass, not engine.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No AIOS commands found.

AIOS Glass reads the live `commands/` directory of your AIOS framework — it never hardcodes the list.

Set the framework path so it can find them.
[Set framework path](command:aios.setFrameworkPath)

aiosGlass.frameworkPath.markdownDescription: Path to your AIOS framework root (the folder containing `plugins/aios/commands/`). `~` is expanded to your home directory. If empty, AIOS Glass falls back to the open workspace root.

aiosGlass.claudeCommand.markdownDescription: The command used to launch native Claude Code. Rituals run as `<claudeCommand> "/aios:<ritual>"` in an integrated terminal.

enumDescriptions: Rendered Markdown preview (Foam renders [[wikilinks]]) — Obsidian-style reading view
Preview opened beside the source editor
aiosGlass.openNotesIn.markdownDescription: How clicking a day in the Calendar opens its note. ⌘/Ctrl-click always opens the source editor; newly-created notes always open in the editor.

enumDescriptions: Ask each time which terminal to use (existing ones, or a new one)
Use the focused terminal (assumed to have a live Claude session), else open a new one
aiosGlass.terminalMode.markdownDescription: Where commands & skills run. (Agents, workers, and resume always open a new terminal.) A new terminal runs `claude "/slash"`; an existing/active one gets the bare `/slash` typed into the Claude session there.

aiosGlass.showHints.markdownDescription: Show the secondary hint texts in the Home panel — button hints (e.g. `browse · task`) and header subtitles (e.g. `discipline compounds`). Turn off for a cleaner, label-only view. Toggle from the cog menu. Counts and helper lines are unaffected.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with AIOS Glass
walkthroughs.description: Operate your AIOS — rituals, agents, calendar, companies — without the terminal.

steps.title: Welcome to AIOS Glass
steps.description:
AIOS Glass is a friendly window onto your AIOS. It surfaces and runs what you already have — slash commands, agents, companies — from clickable panels.

Open the **AIOS** icon in the activity bar anytime.

steps.title: Point Glass at your AIOS
steps.description:
Tell Glass where your framework lives (the folder containing `plugins/aios/commands`).
[Set framework path](command:aios.setFrameworkPath)

steps.title: Open your Home dashboard
steps.description:
Your command center: today's plan, calendar, agents, capabilities, spaces.
[Open AIOS Home](command:aios.openHome)

steps.title: Plan your day
steps.description:
In Home, click **Plan my day** — or run any ritual from the Rituals panel. It launches native Claude for you.
[Open AIOS Home](command:aios.openHome)

steps.title: Spawn an agent
steps.description:
Pick a specialist and a task; Glass spawns a native-Claude worker for you.
[Spawn an agent](command:aios.spawnAgent)

steps.title: Explore your capabilities
steps.description:
Browse the skills, MCPs and plugins your AIOS can use.
[Browse capabilities](command:aios.capabilities.focus)

steps.title: Companies & collaboration
steps.description:
Mount a company, sync context, or open a collaboration space — no flags to memorize.
[Company actions](command:aios.companyAction)

steps.title: Brand-new clone? Run the full interview
steps.description:
If this is a fresh AIOS, run `/aios:cold-start-interview` from the Rituals panel to set everything up end-to-end.
[Open AIOS Home](command:aios.openHome)
